prompt: Implement to_roman(n) in roman.py per its docstring: convert an integer in 1..3999 to a Roman numeral using standard subtractive notation (e.g. 4='IV', 9='IX', 40='XL', 90='XC', 400='CD', 900='CM'), and raise ValueError for anything outside 1..3999. There are NO tests in the repo — reason carefully about edge cases and verify your own work before finishing.
